front: If <InlineFormula>\mathrm{Var}(\tau) \sim \tau^{2H}</InlineFormula>, <InlineFormula>\mathrm{Var}(10)=250</InlineFormula>, and <InlineFormula>\mathrm{Var}(20)=1000</InlineFormula>, then the Hurst exponent is <Blank id="1"></Blank>.
back:

Reasoning: the variance quadruples when the horizon doubles, which implies <InlineFormula>H=1</InlineFormula>.

front: For an ARCH(2) model with <InlineFormula>\omega = 5.528 \times 10^{-4}</InlineFormula>, <InlineFormula>\alpha_1 = 0.315</InlineFormula>, <InlineFormula>\alpha_2 = 0.205</InlineFormula>, <InlineFormula>a_{t-1} = 0.04</InlineFormula>, and <InlineFormula>a_{t-2} = -0.03</InlineFormula>, the conditional variance update is about <Blank id="1"></Blank>.
back:
<Answer id="1">0.00124</Answer>

<BlockFormula>\sigma_t^2 = \omega + \alpha_1 a_{t-1}^2 + \alpha_2 a_{t-2}^2</BlockFormula>
<BlockFormula>= 5.528 \times 10^{-4} + 0.315 \times 0.04^2 + 0.205 \times (-0.03)^2</BlockFormula>
<BlockFormula>= 0.0005528 + 0.000504 + 0.0001845 = 0.0012413 \approx 0.00124</BlockFormula>
Reasoning: this is the direct calculation implied by the printed parameters, even though the original multiple-choice options appear inconsistent with it.

front: For the same ARCH(2) model with <InlineFormula>\omega = 5.528 \times 10^{-4}</InlineFormula>, <InlineFormula>\alpha_1 = 0.315</InlineFormula>, and <InlineFormula>\alpha_2 = 0.205</InlineFormula>, the unconditional variance of <InlineFormula>a_t</InlineFormula> is about <Blank id="1"></Blank>.
back:
<Answer id="1">0.00115</Answer>

<BlockFormula>\mathrm{Var}(a_t) = \frac{\omega}{1 - \alpha_1 - \alpha_2} = \frac{5.528 \times 10^{-4}}{1 - 0.315 - 0.205}</BlockFormula>
<BlockFormula>= \frac{5.528 \times 10^{-4}}{0.48} \approx 0.00115</BlockFormula>
Reasoning: the ARCH coefficients sum to 0.52, so the denominator remains positive and the unconditional variance exists.

front: For the price path <InlineFormula>100, 95, 104, 102, 106, 103, 102, 105</InlineFormula>, the maximum drawdown is <Blank id="1"></Blank>.

front: For Stock A with beta <InlineFormula>1.5</InlineFormula>, risk-free rate <InlineFormula>3\%</InlineFormula>, and market return <InlineFormula>10\%</InlineFormula>, the CAPM expected return is <Blank id="1"></Blank>.
back:
<Answer id="1">13.5%</Answer>

front: For monthly returns <InlineFormula>2.0\%, 1.5\%, -1.0\%, 3.0\%, 0.5\%</InlineFormula> and monthly risk-free rate <InlineFormula>0.5\%</InlineFormula>, the annualized Sharpe ratio is about <Blank id="1"></Blank>.
back:

<BlockFormula>\text{Excess return} = 1.2\% - 0.5\% = 0.7\%</BlockFormula>

front: For Fund A with annual return <InlineFormula>19\%</InlineFormula>, beta <InlineFormula>0.9</InlineFormula>, and market return <InlineFormula>22\%</InlineFormula>, and Fund B with annual return <InlineFormula>15\%</InlineFormula>, beta <InlineFormula>0.5</InlineFormula>, and the same <InlineFormula>4\%</InlineFormula> risk-free rate, the Jensen alphas are <Blank id="1"></Blank> and <Blank id="2"></Blank>.
back:

front: For Asset X with <InlineFormula>\sigma_X = 10\%</InlineFormula>, Asset Y with <InlineFormula>\sigma_Y = 15\%</InlineFormula>, and correlation <InlineFormula>\rho_{XY} = 0.3</InlineFormula>, the GMVP weights are Asset X <Blank id="1"></Blank> and Asset Y <Blank id="2"></Blank>.
